product: Cisco Meraki Dashboard API v1 — organization inventory

auth.note: v1 bearer auth on every request; v0's X-Cisco-Meraki-API-Key header is obsolete. The key must never appear in logs or error text.

query_conventions.arrays: array-valued query parameters are sent as repeated name[] pairs, e.g. productTypes[]=switch&productTypes[]=wireless

pagination.style: RFC 5988 Link response header with up to first/prev/next/last relations
pagination.rel_forms: rel may appear quoted (rel="next") or unquoted (rel=next); both must parse
pagination.rule: follow the rel=next URL verbatim; never rebuild or re-append query parameters

rate_limit.budget: 10 requests per second per organization (burst: 30 in 2 seconds)

headers.Retry-After: seconds to wait before the next request

errors: API rate limit exceeded for organization

rate_limit.client_rule: wait exactly Retry-After seconds via the injected sleep, retry at most max_retries times, then raise carrying status 429

errors.client_rule: raise MerakiAPIError with .status and .errors; never include the API key

success.body: array of {id, name, url, api.enabled, licensing.model, cloud.region.name, management}

list_organizations.client_rule: organizations with api.enabled=false are skipped entirely (no further requests for them)

success.body: array of {id, organizationId, name, productTypes, timeZone, tags, ...}

list_organization_device_availabilities.note: CURRENT replacement for the deprecated GET /organizations/{organizationId}/devices/statuses operation

query.productTypes[]: repeated; e.g. wireless, appliance, switch, camera, cellularGateway, sensor, wirelessController, campusGateway
query.statuses[]: repeated; one of online, alerting, offline, dormant

success.body: array of {mac, name, network: {id}, productType, serial, status, tags}

traversal_contract.order: fetch all organization pages first; then for each API-enabled organization in ascending id order, list networks then device availabilities

output.shape: {organizations: [{id, name, region, networks: [{id, name, productTypes(sorted)}] sorted by id, devices: [{serial, name, mac, productType, status, networkId}] sorted by serial}]}

mock_fixture.pagination_marker: cursorcheck query parameter proves Link URLs are followed verbatim
